name: Mitchell E. Chessman, Bankruptcy Attorney
description_short: Mitchell E. Chessman is a Nashville-based bankruptcy attorney specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings with over a decade of consumer bankruptcy law experience.
description_long:
Mitchell E. Chessman operates a solo bankruptcy law practice in Nashville, Tennessee, focusing exclusively on consumer bankruptcy relief. The firm is registered as a debt relief agency under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code, helping individuals navigate the formal bankruptcy process. Mitchell earned his B.A. from Vanderbilt University in 2005 and his J.D. from the University of Memphis School of Law in 2008, bringing formal legal training and over ten years of specialized bankruptcy practice experience to client cases.

The firm offers Chapter 7 bankruptcy filings (liquidation/fresh start) and Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings (debt reorganization and repayment plans) for individual consumers. Mitchell's approach emphasizes that every bankruptcy case is unique and requires individual attention to detail. The attorney personally ensures each client receives dedicated guidance through each step of the bankruptcy filing process toward achieving a fresh financial start. Services are delivered from a physical office location in Nashville, with direct phone and email contact available.

What distinguishes Mitchell E. Chessman's practice is his stated philosophy that bankruptcy law produces constructive life outcomes for debtors seeking relief. Rather than treating cases as routine filings, the attorney emphasizes personalized case management and detailed attention throughout the process. His decade-plus focus specifically on consumer bankruptcy (rather than general law practice) indicates deep specialization in this narrow field. The practice maintains a professional website with clear contact information and educational content about bankruptcy options.

The primary honest assessment is that this is a solo practitioner with no visible testimonials, case outcomes, pricing information, or online reviews on the website itself. While the attorney's credentials are legitimate and his specialization clear, potential clients cannot independently verify results, client satisfaction, or cost expectations from available materials. The practice appears modest in scale—a single attorney operation rather than a larger firm—which may appeal to clients seeking personalized attention but limits operational capacity.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Over a decade of specialized focus on consumer bankruptcy law rather than general practice
Licensed attorney with formal legal credentials (Vanderbilt B.A., University of Memphis J.D.)
Handles both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, giving clients two debt relief pathways
Emphasizes individualized case management and attention to detail for each client
Registered debt relief agency compliant with U.S. Bankruptcy Code requirements
Direct contact options available (phone, email, physical office address)
Transparent disclosure of debt relief agency status on website
cons: No client testimonials, reviews, or case outcomes visible on website
Solo practitioner operation with limited capacity compared to larger bankruptcy firms
No pricing, fee structure, or cost estimates provided on website
No information about success rates, average debt reduction, or typical outcomes
Limited online presence and absence of Google reviews or third-party rating platforms visible
best_for: Individual consumers in Tennessee (Nashville-area) facing overwhelming debt seeking personalized legal guidance
Debtors unsure whether Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 filing is appropriate for their financial situation
Clients who prefer working with a solo attorney rather than a large law firm
Nashville-area residents who can meet in person at the physical office location
diagnosis: Mitchell E. Chessman is best for individual Tennessee consumers who need bankruptcy legal representation and prefer working with a specialized, credentialed attorney who emphasizes personalized attention. The primary caveat is the absence of visible client reviews, pricing information, or documented outcomes on the website, which limits ability to independently assess results or cost-effectiveness before initial consultation.
services: Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing (liquidation and fresh start)
Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing (debt reorganization and repayment plans)
Debt relief agency representation under U.S. Bankruptcy Code
Step-by-step guidance through bankruptcy filing process
Personalized case management and attention to detail
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
